children.heading: Merger into Capital Fund
children.label: a Merger into Capital Fund
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998, any assistance made available for public housing under section 1437 of this title before , shall be merged into the Capital Fund established under subsection (d).lOctober 1, 1999

children.heading: Merger into Operating Fund
children.label: b Merger into Operating Fund
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998, any assistance made available for public housing under this section before , shall be merged into the Operating Fund established under subsection (e).October 1, 1999

children.label: A
children.text: for assistance from the Capital Fund, the amount determined for the agency under the formula under subsection (d)(2); and

children.label: B
children.text: for assistance from the Operating Fund, the amount determined for the agency under the formula under subsection (e)(2).

children.heading: Capital Fund
children.label: A Capital Fund
children.text: For allocations of assistance from the Capital Fund, $3,000,000,000 for fiscal year 1999, and such sums as may be necessary for fiscal years 2000, 2001, 2002, and 2003.

children.heading: Operating Fund
children.label: B Operating Fund
children.text: For allocations of assistance from the Operating Fund, $2,900,000,000 for fiscal year 1999, and such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 2000, 2001, 2002, and 2003.

children.label: A
children.text: the development, financing, and modernization of public housing projects, including the redesign, reconstruction, and reconfiguration of public housing sites and buildings (including accessibility improvements) and the development of mixed-finance projects;

children.label: B
children.text: vacancy reduction;

children.label: C
children.text: addressing deferred maintenance needs and the replacement of obsolete utility systems and dwelling equipment;

children.label: D
children.text: planned code compliance;

children.label: E
children.text: management improvements, including the establishment and initial operation of computer centers in and around public housing through a Neighborhood Networks initiative, for the purpose of enhancing the self-sufficiency, employability, and economic self-reliance of public housing residents by providing them with onsite computer access and training resources;

children.label: F
children.text: demolition and replacement;

children.label: G
children.text: resident relocation;

children.label: H
children.text: capital expenditures to facilitate programs to improve the empowerment and economic self-sufficiency of public housing residents and to improve resident participation;

children.label: I
children.text: capital expenditures to improve the security and safety of residents;

children.label: J
children.text: homeownership activities, including programs under ;section 1437z–4 of this title

children.label: K
children.text: improvement of energy and water-use efficiency by installing fixtures and fittings that conform to the American Society of Mechanical Engineers/American National Standards Institute standards A112.19.2–1998 and A112.18.1–2000, or any revision thereto, applicable at the time of installation, and by increasing energy efficiency and water conservation by such other means as the Secretary determines are appropriate; and

children.label: L
children.text: integrated utility management and capital planning to maximize energy conservation and efficiency measures.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of public housing dwelling units owned, assisted, or operated by the public housing agency, the characteristics and locations of the projects, and the characteristics of the families served and to be served (including the incomes of the families);

children.label: B
children.text: the need of the public housing agency to carry out rehabilitation and modernization activities, replacement housing, and reconstruction, construction, and demolition activities related to public housing dwelling units owned, assisted, or operated by the public housing agency, including backlog and projected future needs of the agency;

children.label: C
children.text: the cost of constructing and rehabilitating property in the area;

children.label: D
children.text: the need of the public housing agency to carry out activities that provide a safe and secure environment in public housing units owned, assisted, or operated by the public housing agency;

children.label: E
children.text: any record by the public housing agency of exemplary performance in the operation of public housing, as indicated by the system of performance indicators established pursuant to ; andsection 1437d(j) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: any other factors that the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Conditions on use for development and modernization
children.label: 3 Conditions on use for development and modernization

children.heading: Development
children.label: A Development
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this chapter, any public housing developed using amounts provided under this subsection, or under section 1437 of this title as in effect before the effective date under section 503(a) of the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998, shall be operated under the terms and conditions applicable to public housing during the 40-year period that begins on the date on which the project (or stage of the project) becomes available for occupancy.l

children.heading: Modernization
children.label: B Modernization
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this chapter, any public housing or portion thereof that is modernized using amounts provided under this subsection or under section 1437 of this title (as in effect before the effective date under section 503(a) of the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998) shall be maintained and operated under the terms and conditions applicable to public housing during the 20-year period that begins on the latest date on which modernization is completed.l

children.heading: Applicability of latest expiration date
children.label: C Applicability of latest expiration date
children.text: Public housing subject to this paragraph or to any other provision of law mandating the operation of the housing as public housing or under the terms and conditions applicable to public housing for a specified length of time, shall be maintained and operated as required until the latest such expiration date.

children.label: A
children.text: procedures and systems to maintain and ensure the efficient management and operation of public housing units (including amounts sufficient to pay for the reasonable costs of review by an independent auditor of the documentation or other information maintained pursuant to by a public housing agency or resident management corporation to substantiate the performance of that agency or corporation);section 1437d(j)(6) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: activities to ensure a program of routine preventative maintenance;

children.label: C
children.text: anticrime and antidrug activities, including the costs of providing adequate security for public housing residents, including above-baseline police service agreements;

children.label: D
children.text: activities related to the provision of services, including service coordinators for elderly persons or persons with disabilities;

children.label: E
children.text: activities to provide for management and participation in the management and policymaking of public housing by public housing residents;

children.label: F
children.text: the costs of insurance;

children.label: G
children.text: the energy costs associated with public housing units, with an emphasis on energy conservation;

children.label: H
children.text: the costs of administering a public housing work program under , including the costs of any related insurance needs;section 1437j of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the costs of repaying, together with rent contributions, debt incurred to finance the rehabilitation and development of public housing units, which shall be subject to such reasonable requirements as the Secretary may establish;

children.label: J
children.text: the costs associated with the operation and management of mixed finance projects, to the extent appropriate; and

children.label: K
children.text: the costs of operating computer centers in public housing through a Neighborhood Networks initiative described in subsection (d)(1)(E), and of activities related to that initiative.

children.label: i
children.text: standards for the costs of operating and reasonable projections of income, taking into account the characteristics and locations of the public housing projects and characteristics of the families served and to be served (including the incomes of the families), or the costs of providing comparable services as determined in accordance with criteria or a formula representing the operations of a prototype well-managed public housing project;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of public housing dwelling units owned, assisted, or operated by the public housing agency;

children.label: iii
children.text: the number of public housing dwelling units owned, assisted, or operated by the public housing agency that are chronically vacant and the amount of assistance appropriate for those units;

children.label: iv
children.text: to the extent quantifiable, the extent to which the public housing agency provides programs and activities designed to promote the economic self-sufficiency and management skills of public housing residents;

children.label: v
children.text: the need of the public housing agency to carry out anti-crime and anti-drug activities, including providing adequate security for public housing residents;

children.label: vi
children.text: the amount of public housing rental income foregone by the public housing agency as a result of escrow savings accounts under section 1437u(d)(2) of this title for families participating in a family self-sufficiency program of the agency under such ; and2section 1437u of this titleSee References in Text note below.2

children.label: vii
children.text: any other factors that the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Incentive to increase certain rental income
children.label: B Incentive to increase certain rental income
children.text: The formula shall provide an incentive to encourage public housing agencies to facilitate increases in earned income by families in occupancy. Any such incentive shall provide that the agency shall benefit from increases in such rental income and that such amounts accruing to the agency pursuant to such benefit may be used only for low-income housing or to benefit the residents of the public housing agency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The treatment of utility and waste management costs under the formula shall provide that a public housing agency shall receive the full financial benefit from any reduction in the cost of utilities or waste management resulting from any contract with a third party to undertake energy conservation improvements in one or more of its public housing projects.

children.heading: Third party contracts
children.label: ii Third party contracts
children.text: Contracts described in clause (i) may include contracts for equipment conversions to less costly utility sources, projects with resident-paid utilities, and adjustments to frozen base year consumption, including systems repaired to meet applicable building and safety codes and adjustments for occupancy rates increased by rehabilitation.

children.heading: Term of contract
children.label: iii Term of contract
children.text: The total term of a contract described in clause (i) shall not exceed 20 years to allow longer payback periods for retrofits, including windows, heating system replacements, wall insulation, site-based generation, advanced energy savings technologies, including renewable energy generation, and other such retrofits.

children.heading: Existing contracts
children.label: iv Existing contracts
children.text: The term of a contract described in clause (i) that, as of , is in repayment and has a term of not more than 12 years, may be extended to a term of not more than 20 years to permit additional energy conservation improvements without requiring the reprocurement of energy performance contractors.December 26, 2007

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A small public housing agency, as defined in , may elect to be paid for its utility and waste management costs under the formula for a period, at the discretion of the small public housing agency, of not more than 20 years based on the small public housing agency’s average annual consumption during the 3-year period preceding the year in which the election is made (in this subparagraph referred to as the “consumption base level”).section 1437z–10(a) of this title

children.heading: Initial adjustment in consumption base level
children.label: ii Initial adjustment in consumption base level
children.text: The Secretary shall make an initial one-time adjustment in the consumption base level to account for differences in the heating degree day average over the most recent 20-year period compared to the average in the consumption base level.

children.heading: Adjustments in consumption base level
children.label: iii Adjustments in consumption base level
children.text: The Secretary shall make adjustments in the consumption base level to account for an increase or reduction in units, a change in fuel source, a change in resident controlled electricity consumption, or for other reasons.

children.text: shall accrue to the small public housing agency; and

children.label: II
children.text: may be used for any public housing purpose at the discretion of the small public housing agency.

children.label: I
children.text: may use, but shall not be required to use, the services of a third party in its energy conservation program; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall have the sole discretion to determine the source, and terms and conditions, of any financing used for its energy conservation program.

children.heading: Condition on use
children.label: 3 Condition on use
children.text: No portion of any public housing project operated using amounts provided under this subsection, or under this section as in effect before the effective date under section 503(a) of the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998, may be disposed of before the expiration of the 10-year period beginning upon the conclusion of the fiscal year for which such amounts were provided, except as otherwise provided in this chapter.

children.heading: Negotiated rulemaking procedure
children.label: f Negotiated rulemaking procedure
children.text: The formulas under subsections (d)(2) and (e)(2) shall be developed according to procedures for issuance of regulations under the negotiated rulemaking procedure under subchapter III of chapter 5 of title 5.

children.heading: Limitations on use of funds
children.label: g Limitations on use of funds

children.heading: Flexibility for Capital Fund amounts
children.label: A Flexibility for Capital Fund amounts
children.text: Of any amounts appropriated for fiscal year 2000 or any fiscal year thereafter that are allocated for fiscal year 2000 or any fiscal year thereafter from the Capital Fund for any public housing agency, the agency may use not more than 20 percent for activities that are eligible under subsection (e) for assistance with amounts from the Operating Fund, but only if the public housing agency plan for the agency provides for such use.

children.heading: Flexibility for Operating Fund amounts
children.label: B Flexibility for Operating Fund amounts
children.text: Of any amounts appropriated for fiscal year 2016 or any fiscal year thereafter that are allocated for fiscal year 2016 or any fiscal year thereafter from the Operating Fund for any public housing agency, the agency may use not more than 20 percent for activities that are eligible under subsection (d) for assistance with amounts from the Capital Fund, but only if the public housing plan under for the agency provides for such use.section 1437c–1 of this title

children.heading: Full flexibility for small PHAs
children.label: 2 Full flexibility for small PHAs
children.text: Of any amounts allocated for any fiscal year for any public housing agency that owns or operates less than 250 public housing dwelling units, is not designated pursuant to as a troubled public housing agency, and (in the determination of the Secretary) is operating and maintaining its public housing in a safe, clean, and healthy condition, the agency may use any such amounts for any eligible activities under subsections (d)(1) and (e)(1), regardless of the fund from which the amounts were allocated and provided. This subsection shall take effect on .section 1437d(j)(2) of this titleOctober 21, 1998

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), a public housing agency may not use any of the amounts allocated for the agency from the Capital Fund or Operating Fund for the purpose of constructing any public housing unit, if such construction would result in a net increase from the number of public housing units owned, assisted, or operated by the public housing agency on , including any public housing units demolished as part of any revitalization effort.October 1, 1999

children.heading: Exception regarding use of assistance
children.label: B Exception regarding use of assistance
children.text: A public housing agency may use amounts allocated for the agency from the Capital Fund or Operating Fund for the construction and operation of housing units that are available and affordable to low-income families in excess of the limitations on new construction set forth in subparagraph (A), but the formulas established under subsections (d)(2) and (e)(2) shall not provide additional funding for the specific purpose of allowing construction and operation of housing in excess of those limitations (except to the extent provided in subparagraph (C)).

children.label: ii
children.text: the estimated cost of the useful life of the project is less than the estimated cost of providing tenant-based assistance under section 1437f() of this title for the same period of time.o

children.heading: Eligibility of units acquired from proceeds of sales under demolition or disposition plan
children.label: i Eligibility of units acquired from proceeds of sales under demolition or disposition plan
children.text: If a public housing agency uses proceeds from the sale of units under a homeownership program in accordance with to acquire additional units to be sold to low-income families, the additional units shall be counted as public housing for purposes of determining the amount of the allocation to the agency under this section until sale by the agency, but in no case longer than 5 years.section 1437z–4 of this title

children.heading: Penalty for slow expenditure of capital funds
children.label: j Penalty for slow expenditure of capital funds
A
children.text: the date on which the funds become available to the agency for obligation in the case of modernization; or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which the agency accumulates adequate funds to undertake modernization, substantial rehabilitation, or new construction of units.

children.heading: Extension of time period for obligation
children.label: 2 Extension of time period for obligation
i
children.text: litigation;

children.label: ii
children.text: obtaining approvals of the Federal Government or a State or local government;

children.label: iii
children.text: complying with environmental assessment and abatement requirements;

children.label: iv
children.text: relocating residents;

children.label: v
children.text: an event beyond the control of the public housing agency; or

children.label: vi
children.text: any other reason established by the Secretary by notice published in the Federal Register;

children.label: B
children.text: shall disregard the requirements of paragraph (1) with respect to any unobligated amounts made available to a public housing agency, to the extent that the total of such amounts does not exceed 10 percent of the original amount made available to the public housing agency; and

children.label: i
children.text: the size of the public housing agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: the complexity of capital program of the public housing agency;

children.label: iii
children.text: any limitation on the ability of the public housing agency to obligate the amounts allocated for the agency from the Capital Fund in a timely manner as a result of State or local law; or

children.label: iv
children.text: such other factors as the Secretary determines to be relevant.

children.heading: Prohibition of new assistance
children.label: A Prohibition of new assistance
children.text: A public housing agency shall not be awarded assistance under this section for any month during any fiscal year in which the public housing agency has funds unobligated in violation of paragraph (1) or (2).

children.heading: Withholding of assistance
children.label: B Withholding of assistance
children.text: During any fiscal year described in subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall withhold all assistance that would otherwise be provided to the public housing agency. If the public housing agency cures its failure to comply during the year, it shall be provided with the share attributable to the months remaining in the year.

children.heading: Redistribution
children.label: C Redistribution
children.text: The total amount of any funds not provided public housing agencies by operation of this paragraph shall be allocated for agencies determined under to be high-performing.section 1437d(j) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), if the Secretary has consented, before the effective date under section 503(a) of the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998, to an obligation period for any agency longer than provided under paragraph (1), a public housing agency that obligates its funds before the expiration of that period shall not be considered to be in violation of paragraph (1).

children.heading: Prior fiscal years
children.label: B Prior fiscal years
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), any funds appropriated to a public housing agency for fiscal year 1997 or prior fiscal years shall be fully obligated by the public housing agency not later than .September 30, 1999

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A public housing agency shall spend any assistance received under this section not later than 4 years (plus the period of any extension approved by the Secretary under paragraph (2)) after the date on which funds become available to the agency for obligation.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: B Enforcement
children.text: The Secretary shall enforce the requirement of subparagraph (A) through default remedies up to and including withdrawal of the funding.

children.heading: Right of recapture
children.label: 6 Right of recapture
children.text: Any obligation entered into by a public housing agency shall be subject to the right of the Secretary to recapture the obligated amounts for violation by the public housing agency of the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: Treatment of replacement reserve
children.label: 7 Treatment of replacement reserve
children.text: The requirements of this subsection shall not apply to funds held in replacement reserves established pursuant to subsection (n).

children.heading: Treatment of nonrental income
children.label: k Treatment of nonrental income
children.text: A public housing agency that receives income from nonrental sources (as determined by the Secretary) may retain and use such amounts without any decrease in the amounts received under this section from the Capital or Operating Fund. Any such nonrental amounts retained shall be used only for low-income housing or to benefit the residents assisted by the public housing agency.

children.heading: Provision of only capital or operating assistance
children.label: l Provision of only capital or operating assistance

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 1 Authority
children.text: In appropriate circumstances, as determined by the Secretary, a public housing agency may commit capital assistance only, or operating assistance only, for public housing units, which assistance shall be subject to all of the requirements applicable to public housing except as otherwise provided in this subsection.

children.heading: Exemptions
children.label: 2 Exemptions
children.text: In the case of any public housing unit assisted pursuant to the authority under paragraph (1), the Secretary may, by regulation, reduce the period under subsection (d)(3) or (e)(3), as applicable, during which such units must be operated under requirements applicable to public housing. In cases in which there is commitment of operating assistance but no commitment of capital assistance, the Secretary may make section 8 [] requirements applicable, as appropriate, by regulation.42 U.S.C. 1437f

children.heading: Reduction of asthma incidence
children.label: 2 Reduction of asthma incidence
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, the New York City Housing Authority may, in its sole discretion, from amounts provided from the Operating and Capital Funds, or from amounts provided for public housing before amounts are made available from such Funds, use not more than exceeding $500,000 per year for the purpose of initiating, expanding or continuing a program for the reduction of the incidence of asthma among residents. The Secretary shall consult with the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency and the Secretary of Health and Human Services to identify and consider sources of funding for the reduction of the incidence of asthma among recipients of assistance under this subchapter.3So in original.3

children.heading: Services for elderly residents
children.label: 3 Services for elderly residents
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, the New York City Housing Authority may, in its sole discretion, from amounts provided from the Operating and Capital Funds, or from amounts provided for public housing before the amounts are made available from such Funds, use not more than $600,000 per year for the purpose of developing a comprehensive plan to address the need for services for elderly residents. Such plan may be developed by a partnership created by such Housing Authority and may include the creation of a model project for assisted living at one or more developments. The model project may provide for contracting with private parties for the delivery of services.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 4 Effective date
children.text: This subsection shall apply to fiscal year 1999 and each fiscal year thereafter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Public housing agencies shall be permitted to establish a replacement reserve to fund any of the capital activities listed in subsection (d)(1).

children.heading: Source and amount of funds for replacement reserve
children.label: 2 Source and amount of funds for replacement reserve
A
children.text: At the discretion of the Secretary, public housing agencies may transfer and hold in a replacement reserve funds originating from additional sources.

children.label: B
children.text: No minimum transfer of funds to a replacement reserve shall be required.

children.label: C
children.text: At any time, a public housing agency may not hold in a replacement reserve more than the amount the public housing authority has determined necessary to satisfy the anticipated capital needs of properties in its portfolio assisted under this section, as outlined in its Capital Fund 5-Year Action Plan, or a comparable plan, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: D
children.text: The Secretary may establish, by regulation, a maximum replacement reserve level or levels that are below amounts determined under subparagraph (C), which may be based upon the size of the portfolio assisted under this section or other factors.

children.heading: Transfer of operating funds
children.label: 3 Transfer of operating funds
children.text: In first establishing a replacement reserve, the Secretary may allow public housing agencies to transfer more than 20 percent of its operating funds into its replacement reserve.

children.heading: Expenditure
children.label: 4 Expenditure
children.text: Funds in a replacement reserve may be used for purposes authorized by subsection (d)(1) and contained in its Capital Fund 5-Year Action Plan.

children.heading: Management and report
children.label: 5 Management and report
children.text: The Secretary shall establish appropriate accounting and reporting requirements to ensure that public housing agencies are spending funds on eligible projects and that funds in the replacement reserve are connected to capital needs.

children.heading: Public housing heating guidelines
children.label: o Public housing heating guidelines
children.text: The Secretary shall publish model guidelines for minimum heating requirements for public housing dwelling units operated by public housing agencies receiving assistance under this section.
